title: Saren Llalik

{{Quote|The Alliance is very fortunate to have the assistance of Dr. Llalik. ''[…]'' Through her work, she has been the savior of countless Rebel lives.|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]|<ref name="CRO" />}}
[[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] '''Saren Llalik''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] technology researcher from [[Plavin 6]]. As an authority in cyborg technology, Llalik was responsible for several quality products such as the [[Borg Construct Aj^6]]. As a secret agent of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], she also developed an [[Spy/Legends|espionage]] system to provide the Alliance with sensitive and top-secret information about new technologies developed in the company she worked for, [[BioTech Industries/Legends|BioTech Industries]]. Llalik's work saved the lives of many [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel soldiers]], and Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] considered himself to be in debt to Llalik.

Llalik was also the younger sister of Doctor [[Nashiak Llalik]], a developer of [[Armor/Legends|armor]] technology who, unlike his sister, chose to work for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. In [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], Llalik wrote reports for [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] on her brother and another [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] and potential threat to Rebel activities, Doctor [[Cinto Alaras]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|While the Empire holds an advantage in manufacturing capability, the Alliance has teams of tremendously innovative specialist, technicians and engineers — people like General Jan Dodonna, General Airen Cracken and Doctor Saren Llalik — who have been able to create new devices or re-engineer existing items to give the Alliance's soldiers an edge in combat.|[[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]], soon after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]|<ref name="tbsb">''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]''</ref>}}
Born on the humid [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Plavin 6]], Saren Llalik was the third child and only daughter of [[Doctor/Legends|Doctors]] [[Nash Llalik|Nash]] and [[Loran Llalik]]. Her elder brother and middle child of the family, [[Nashiak Llalik]], interested in [[Armor/Legends|armors]] from a young age, entertained Saren during their childhood by telling her tales about the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] and the [[Sun Guard]], and their respective armors. Saren and her [[Unidentified Llalik child|oldest brother]] joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] around the same time that Nashiak joined [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]], an umbrella organization of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="air" /> Saren, however, hid her affiliation so she could work as a secret agent.<ref name="CRO" /> Both Saren and Nashiak became gurus in their respective fields of expertise: Saren as a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] technology expert, and Nashiak as an armor technology expert.<ref name="air" />

By [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Llalik had become the [[Director of Prosthetic Design]] for [[BioTech Industries/Legends|BioTech Industries]], a corporation dealing in medical and cyborg technologies. As a [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] specialist working in different experimental designs, she was reputable for the high quality design projects she directed and managed, including the cybernetic implant [[Borg Construct Aj^6]] unit. Llalik worked from a [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratory]] in a restricted area of BioTech's headquarters in the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]], and she also developed a system to provide top-secret information to the [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Rebel command]], including military designs for the Empire, using an unwitting [[2-1B surgical droid/Legends|2-1B surgical droid]] at her lab. Llalik had included an [[Espionage droid|espionage module]] in the [[Droid/Legends|droid]], using it to store files about new cybernetic designs. Medical [[Technician/Legends|technician]] [[Kena Shont]], also a Rebel agent, later downloaded the files onto her personal [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and gave the information to the Rebel command. As BioTech maintenance could not detect the [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] unless they performed an analysis that only the Director of Prosthetic Design—Llalik herself—could request, her work remained undiscovered. Through her work for the Alliance, mostly by providing the Alliance with secret information and useful new technological designs, Llalik saved many lives and earned the respect of Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], who considered himself to be in debt to her and even included her in his own contact list.<ref name="CRO" />

In [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], while Llalik was on an extended paid vacation, she helped the [[Task Force on Alliance Security]] as a consultant on medical and cyborg advances that could be used against the Empire. In that role, she wrote a report on the threat posed by Doctor [[Cinto Alaras]], an [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] with no known [[Politics/Legends|political]] affiliation, and on Rebel agent [[Sommen]], who was working undercover for the same corporation as Alaras. Llalik also wrote a report on her own brother Nashiak, who had become an armor designer for Imperial military projects.<ref name="air" />

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Llalik was still active as a member of the soon-to-be [[Alliance of Free Planets]]. At that point, she was well-respected because of her engineering work that provided the [[Rebel trooper/Legends|soldiers]] of the Alliance with new technologies. Alliance historian [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] compared the importance of her work with that of Generals [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] and Airen Cracken.<ref name="tbsb" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I would very much like him to be recruited to the Alliance's side, not only to help us in our struggle, but so I could hear him tell one of his stories again…|Saren Llalik, on [[Nashiak Llalik]]|<ref name="air" />}}
Saren Llalik had advanced technical skills in several fields, including the programming, design, and maintenance of [[Computer/Legends|computers]], droids, and prosthetics, as well as management, medicine, and cyborgs. She was also knowledgeable in other skills, including [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], security, and even forgery. Llalik underwent training to increase her physical endurance, and although she knew how to use a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and even evade a shot, she rarely carried any weapon. Her equipment included a datapad, a diagnostic computer, a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and a [[Medpac/Legends|medpac]], along with an ID to access her own lab.<ref name="CRO" />

Llalik was very fond of her older brother Nashiak, and she believed him to be a very good engineer in his field. Not wanting to think badly of him, she also believed he was simply blind to the evil uses of his projects, and not really as cruel as his employers. She also wished that Nashiak would join the Alliance so that she could work with him and share their free time together.<ref name="air" />

==Behind the scenes==
Saren Llalik was first mentioned in the [[roleplaying game]] book ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'' ([[1994]]), published by [[West End Games]], edited by [[Bill Smith]], and written by several authors. The book included a graphical depiction of Llalik by [[Brian Schomburg]]. Llalik was also mentioned in two other roleplaying books by the same company and co-written by Smith, ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' ([[1995]]) and ''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'' ([[1996]]).
